sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשֹּׁךְ Ecc 10:11, and יִשַּׁךְ Pro 23:32, to bite, as a serpent, Gen 49:17 Num 21:6, seq. [Piel]; as a man, Mic 3:5 (Eth. ነሰከ፡ id.; Syriac transp, ܢܟܬܰ); metaph.-(a) to vex, to oppress, Hab 2:7.-(b) to lend on usury, Deu 23:20 since not only lending on usury, but even receiving interest was supposed to mark a sordid person and an oppressor of the weak (comp. Aram. נְכַת, ܢܟܬܰ to bite; whence נֹוכְתּא usury; Arab. قرض to gnaw; Conj. III. to lend on usury; Gr. δάκνεσθαι ὑπὸ τῶν χρεῶν, Arist. Nub. i. 12; Lat. usura vorax, Lucan, i. 171).

Piel, i.q. Kal, to bite, Num 21:6 Jer 8:17.

Hiphil הִשִּׁיךְ to take usury of any one, followed by a dat. of pers. Deu 23:21. Hence-
